xEnrich 0.1.0

Classify selected pathways by signal strength and independence

Source: R/classify_assoc_selection.R
classify_assoc_selection.Rd

Adds interpretive classification columns to the output of run_assoc_redundancy_selection, helping users distinguish primary drivers from secondary signals, redundant shadows, and potential artifacts.

classify_assoc_selection(
  sel_result,
  z_strong = 10,
  z_moderate = 5,
  ratio_primary = 0.7,
  ratio_independent = 0.5,
  ratio_partial = 0.35
)

Arguments

sel_result

Data frame returned by run_assoc_redundancy_selection.

z_strong

Numeric. z-score threshold for "strong" signal. Default 10.

z_moderate

Numeric. z-score threshold for "moderate" signal. Default 5.

ratio_primary

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "primary" class. Default 0.70.

ratio_independent

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "independent" class. Default 0.50.

ratio_partial

Numeric in (0, 1). Minimum redundancy_ratio for "partial" class (below = "redundant"). Default 0.35.

Value

The input data frame with three additional columns appended:

marginal_strength

Ordered factor: "strong", "moderate", "weak".

signal_class

Ordered factor: "primary", "independent", "partial", "redundant", "suppressor".

action

Character. Plain-language reporting recommendation.

Classification logic

Two orthogonal axes are assessed independently and then combined:

Signal strength (marginal_strength) reflects how robustly a pathway associates with the phenotype, based on its marginal z-score from run_info_assoc:

  • "strong": z-score >= z_strong. Robust signal, replicable across permutations.

  • "moderate": z-score >= z_moderate. Reliable but sensitive to sample size.

  • "weak": z-score < z_moderate. Treat cautiously regardless of independence.

Independence class (signal_class) reflects how much independent information the pathway contributes beyond earlier-selected pathways, based on redundancy_ratio and the is_suppressor flag:

  • "primary": ratio >= ratio_primary AND z-score >= z_strong. Strong, independent signal. Lead finding.

  • "independent": ratio >= ratio_independent, not primary. Genuine secondary biology worth reporting.

  • "partial": ratio >= ratio_partial, not above. Contributing signal, not fully independent. Mention as correlated with earlier selections.

  • "redundant": ratio < ratio_partial. Largely captured by earlier pathways. Dismiss from primary reporting.

  • "suppressor": is_suppressor == TRUE. Conditional MI exceeded marginal MI after orthogonalisation – selection was driven by noise interaction with already-selected pathways. Inspect before reporting.

Note that "suppressor" takes precedence over all other classes since it signals a potential artifact regardless of ratio magnitude.

Threshold guidance

Defaults are calibrated for typical omics datasets (n = 100-500 samples, Reactome pathway gene sets). Adjust for your context:

  • Noisier data (n < 100): lower z_strong to 7, z_moderate to 3.

  • Many correlated pathways (e.g. GO terms): raise ratio_independent to 0.6.

  • Conservative reporting: raise ratio_primary to 0.8.

See also

run_assoc_redundancy_selection for the upstream selection step, plot_gains to visualise the selection profile.

Examples

set.seed(42)
n_samples <- 100
genes     <- paste0("G", 1:300)
factor_A  <- rnorm(n_samples)
factor_B  <- rnorm(n_samples)
expr      <- matrix(
  rnorm(n_samples * length(genes)),
  nrow     = n_samples,
  dimnames = list(NULL, genes)
)
expr[, 1:30]    <- expr[, 1:30]    + factor_A
expr[, 200:230] <- expr[, 200:230] + factor_B
phenotype <- 0.7 * factor_A + 0.5 * factor_B + rnorm(n_samples, sd = 0.4)
gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A  = genes[1:30],
  pathway_A2 = genes[5:35],
  pathway_B  = genes[200:230],
  pathway_C  = genes[100:130]
)
assoc <- run_info_assoc(
  expr = expr, phenotype = phenotype,
  gene_sets = gene_sets, n_perm = 500, seed = 42
)
#> Auto-selected nbins = 4 (Rice rule, capped for joint-table density and 20).
sel <- run_assoc_redundancy_selection(
  assoc_results = assoc, expr = expr, phenotype = phenotype,
  gene_sets = gene_sets, seed = 42
)
#> 3 / 4 pathways significant at padj <= 0.05.
#> Auto-selected nbins = 4.
#> Auto-selected min_gain = 0.08820 bits (noise floor = 0.08820, relevance = 0.06828 [15% of leading MI]).
#> Stopping at step 3: best CMI = 0.00000 bits < min_gain = 0.08820.
classified <- classify_assoc_selection(sel)
print(classified[, c("pathway", "marginal_strength", "signal_class", "action")])
#>             pathway marginal_strength signal_class              action
#> pathway_A pathway_A            strong      primary              report
#> pathway_B pathway_B          moderate  independent report as secondary
